items.name: Ableton Live MCP
items.summary: MCP server that lets Codex control Ableton Live.
items.why_included: HN commenters treated it as a real AI-assisted music-production workflow, not just a toy.
items.evidence: The thread discussed voice-driven composition, arrangement edits, dynamics changes, and iterative feedback inside Ableton with Codex/Claude.

items.name: Text-to-CAD
items.summary: Prompt-driven CAD pipeline that turns text into 3D geometry.
items.why_included: Commenters kept comparing it to other serious text-to-CAD efforts and practical CAD backends.
items.evidence: Replies mentioned Claude generating OpenSCAD, a separate cordyceps repo, and zoo.dev as a serious benchmark.

items.name: XSecureLock
items.summary: Crash-safe X11 screen locker built for secure desktop locking.
items.why_included: A commenter explicitly recommended it as the safer lock tool for custom desktop setups.
items.evidence: The recommendation cited multiprocess design and avoiding an unprotected desktop if the locker crashes.

items.name: Apple SHARP
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Apple's SHARP running in the browser via ONNX runtime web
items.summary: Browser demo that runs Apple's SHARP 3D model locally.
items.why_included: Commenters liked the client-side privacy story and spent time on the runtime limits.
items.evidence: The thread talked about a 2.4 GB ONNX model, WebGPU inference, browser memory crashes, and local-only processing.

items.name: OpenSCAD
items.summary: Script-based CAD modeller people used with Claude for 3D printing.
items.why_included: The comments showed a concrete prompt-to-CAD workflow, not abstract AI hype.
items.evidence: One commenter said they use Claude to generate OpenSCAD for 3D printing; others discussed its limits for spatial reasoning.

items.name: zoo.dev
items.summary: Conversational CAD platform HN cited as a serious text-to-CAD effort.
items.why_included: It came up as the heavyweight reference point for people building text-to-CAD systems.
items.evidence: A commenter said text-to-CAD fans should mention zoo.dev and noted it had gone to extreme lengths on the problem.
